channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines curated by Frontion

item.title: Bank of Japan holds rates steady in split vote as Iran war fuels inflation concerns
item.description: The BOJ kept its benchmark rate at 0.75% but three of nine board members proposed a hike, signaling growing concern over inflationary pressures from Middle East conflict disrupting energy markets.

item.title: King Charles arrives in US for state visit overshadowed by Iran tensions and shooting
item.description: The British monarch's most consequential state visit marks 250 years since US independence, but is being conducted under the shadow of the Iran conflict and the White House Correspondents dinner shooting.

item.title: Israeli strikes expand to eastern Lebanon as ceasefire continues to fray
item.description: The Israeli military expanded its bombing campaign to eastern Lebanon, striking over 20 Hezbollah targets. At least 14 people were killed including two children, as both sides accused each other of violating the US-mediated ceasefire.

item.title: Zelenskiy rejects EU associate membership offer, demands full accession by 2027
item.description: Ukraine's president rejected a German-French proposal for associate membership at a Cyprus summit, insisting on full EU membership by 2027. German Chancellor Merz suggested Ukraine may need to accept territorial losses to advance EU prospects.

item.title: Oil prices climb 1% as Iran war stalemate persists
item.description: Brent crude rose toward $110 per barrel as diplomatic efforts to end the US-Iran war appeared stalled, with the Strait of Hormuz remaining effectively closed to shipping.

item.title: China denounces Japan and EU over South China Sea at UN
item.description: Beijing accused Tokyo of provocative behavior in the Taiwan Strait and military expansion plans after Japan and the EU raised concerns about South China Sea tensions at a UN Security Council meeting.

item.title: Ukraine summons Israeli ambassador over Russian grain shipments
item.description: Kyiv warned Israel about allowing grain shipments from Russian-occupied Ukrainian territories, with Foreign Minister Sybiha saying Israel must prevent "stolen" Ukrainian grain from entering its ports.

item.title: Iran proposes reopening Strait of Hormuz while delaying nuclear talks
item.description: Tehran offered to restore maritime traffic through the vital waterway and lift the US blockade, but suggested postponing the contentious nuclear issue negotiations to a later stage.

item.title: Moody's Upgrades China Outlook to Stable, Cites Economic Resilience
item.description: Credit ratings agency Moody's revised China's outlook from negative to stable, pointing to resilient economic and fiscal strength despite ongoing trade pressures and geopolitical challenges.

item.title: US Pressures Taiwan Parliament to Pass Comprehensive Defense Budget
item.description: The top U.S. diplomat in Taiwan urged the opposition-majority parliament to approve a comprehensive defense budget, emphasizing the critical need for integrated air and missile defense systems amid regional tensions.

item.title: Iran War Hands OPEC's Swing Producer Crown to America
item.description: The United States has emerged as the world's dominant energy superpower, stepping in to shield the global economy from the oil crunch triggered by the Iran conflict through increased exports and strategic reserve releases.

item.title: Italy Extradites Chinese Cyber-Espionage Suspect to US
item.description: Italy has extradited Xu Zewei, a 34-year-old alleged Chinese state-sponsored hacker, to the US where he faces charges for stealing COVID-19 vaccine research during the pandemic as part of the "Hafnium" cyber-espionage campaign targeting universities and immunologists.

item.title: 50+ Nations Launch First-Ever Fossil Fuel Exit Talks in Colombia
item.description: More than 50 governments including major producers like Canada, Norway, Australia and Brazil are meeting in Santa Marta for the inaugural conference on phasing out fossil fuels, bypassing UN climate talks to address the main driver of global warming amid the Iran war energy crunch.

item.title: Japan Seeks Deeper Defense Ties with Australia Amid Indo-Pacific Tensions
item.description: Japan's Defense Minister Shinjiro Koizumi pledged to deepen defense cooperation with Australia at a Nikkei forum in Perth, citing an "increasingly severe" security environment in the Indo-Pacific as regional tensions mount.

item.title: Lebanese Minister Says Hezbollah Disarmament Must Happen
item.description: Lebanon's Social Affairs Minister Haneen Sayed told a CSIS forum in Washington that Hezbollah disarmament "must happen" and is a government demand, but cautioned it would require a political settlement and time given the group's role as a "community" not just a militant organization.

item.title: CATL Shares Drop 7% After Announcing $5 Billion Renewable Expansion Fund
item.description: World's largest EV battery maker CATL announced a HK$39.2 billion (US$5 billion) private placement to expand its renewables business, with shares falling nearly 7% as investors reacted to the dilution amid global oil price surges from the Iran conflict.

item.title: Pakistan Accused of Bombing Kunar University in Afghanistan
item.description: Pakistani air strikes on Kunar province killed at least 7 people and wounded 75, including university students. Taliban condemned the attack as 'war crimes,' with reports of jets, drones, and mortars targeting civilian areas.

item.title: Suspect in Washington Dinner Shooting Charged With Attempted Assassination of Trump
item.description: Cole Tomas Allen, 31, was charged with attempting to assassinate President Trump after opening fire at the White House Correspondents' Dinner. He faces life in prison if convicted.

item.title: Indonesia Train Collision Kills 14 Near Jakarta
item.description: A long-distance train crashed into a commuter train outside Jakarta, killing at least 14 people and injuring 84. Rescue teams worked to free passengers trapped in the women-only carriage. The accident raises fresh concerns about Indonesia's aging rail infrastructure.

item.title: Trump Unhappy With Latest Iranian Proposal, US Official Says
item.description: President Trump expressed dissatisfaction with Iran's latest offer to end the two-month war, as Tehran's proposal did not address nuclear program concerns. Peace talks remain stalled with no breakthrough in sight.

item.title: US and Iran Clash at UN Over Nuclear Non-Proliferation Role
item.description: Tensions flared at the United Nations after Tehran was selected as a vice president for the NPT review conference, prompting a sharp rebuke from Washington over Iran's nuclear program amid the ongoing conflict.

item.title: NATO Considers Ending Annual Summits Amid Trump Tensions
item.description: Six sources told Reuters that NATO is weighing whether to end its practice of annual summits, potentially avoiding a tense encounter with Trump in his final year as questions mount over the alliance's future.

item.title: Russian Drone Strike on Odesa Wounds 14 Including Children
item.description: An overnight Russian drone attack on Ukraine's southern port city wounded over a dozen people, including two children, and damaged residential buildings as the war continues.

item.title: Afghanistan risks losing 25,000 female teachers and health workers by 2030
item.description: UNICEF warns that Taliban restrictions on girls education and women employment could eliminate 25% of Afghanistan 2021 female workforce, affecting 1 million girls currently with projections to double by 2030.

item.title: Regional police crackdown arrests 326 in Asia-wide child abuse probe
item.description: Singapore, Brunei, Hong Kong, Japan, Malaysia, South Korea and Thailand conducted a four-week operation arresting 326 people and investigating 119 more across 382 locations for online child sexual exploitation.
